false_positives_removed: api-004 — /ingest endpoint returns generic 'Error processing directory ingestion' at line 589, NOT str(e). Explorer misread the code.
api-015 — ingest_file is 37 lines, well under 80-line god function threshold. Explorer miscounted.
severity_adjustments: api-001/002 downgraded from CRITICAL to HIGH — localhost-only CORS (4 origins) partially mitigates remote exploitation. Absolute path bypass is intentional for local model path access.
api-003 downgraded from CRITICAL to MEDIUM — tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile on Windows uses counter-based naming in ACL-protected temp directory. Local-only app reduces exploit surface.

findings.title: Absolute paths bypass base_dir boundary check in validate_model_path
findings.problem: validate_model_path allows absolute paths (line 172-174) without verifying they stay within base_dir. The relative_to() check at lines 180-183 only runs in the else branch for relative paths. An absolute path to any system location passes validation. While localhost-only CORS provides partial mitigation, this violates defense-in-depth — any local process can point the API to arbitrary paths.
findings.fix: For model paths: add a configurable allowlist of trusted root directories. For document directories: reject absolute paths entirely or validate against allowlist.

findings.title: Absolute paths bypass base_dir boundary check in validate_directory
findings.problem: Same pattern as api-001 but for document ingestion directory. validate_directory() at lines 190-210 has identical structure: absolute paths resolve without base_dir boundary check. A local attacker or compromised local service could trigger ingestion of arbitrary directories on the filesystem.
findings.fix: Add relative_to() check for absolute paths, or reject absolute paths and require relative paths only.
findings.evidence: Same code pattern as validate_model_path lines 172-183

findings.title: Temp file naming uses counter-based approach
findings.problem: tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile on Windows uses GetTempFileName which generates names via sequential counter (prefix + hex counter). While the temp directory is ACL-protected on modern Windows, this is a defense-in-depth concern. A local attacker with write access to the temp directory could pre-create symlinks at predictable paths.
findings.fix: Use tempfile.mkstemp with a secrets.token_urlsafe() suffix for additional entropy, or use tempfile.TemporaryDirectory with restricted permissions.
findings.ai_pattern: Default library behavior assumed to be secure

findings.title: file.filename is str | None but sanitize_filename expects str — runtime crash
findings.problem: FastAPI's UploadFile.filename is Optional[str]. If a request sends a non-file field or malformed multipart upload, file.filename can be None. sanitize_filename(file.filename) would then call os.path.basename(None), causing TypeError. The LSP already flags this at line 600.
findings.fix: Add None check: if file.filename is None: raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail='Filename is required')
findings.evidence: safe_filename, display_name = sanitize_filename(file.filename)  # file.filename can be None
findings.ai_pattern: Type safety gap — optional types not handled

findings.title: No file size limit on uploaded files — resource exhaustion
findings.problem: file.read() at line 612 is called without size limits. An attacker can upload arbitrarily large files to exhaust server memory. For a local app this is less critical, but a malformed or extremely large upload could cause the API to become unresponsive.
findings.fix: Add max size: content = await file.read(max_size) where max_size is configurable (e.g., 100MB). Check Content-Length header first for early rejection.
findings.evidence: content = await file.read()  # No size limit

findings.title: CORS allows all methods and headers from localhost origins
findings.problem: allow_methods=['*'] and allow_headers=['*'] means any HTTP method (including PUT, PATCH, DELETE) and any header can be sent. While origins are restricted to localhost, if another service on localhost is compromised, the broad CORS permissions provide maximum attack surface.
findings.fix: Explicitly list only required methods: allow_methods=['GET', 'POST'] and allow_headers=['Content-Type', 'Accept'].

findings.title: /ingest/file leaks internal error details via str(e)
findings.problem: Line 625-626 catches all exceptions and returns str(e) directly to the client. This can expose internal file paths, module names, stack trace fragments, and other sensitive information useful for further attacks.
findings.fix: Return generic error message to client, log full exception server-side: raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail='Error processing file')
findings.evidence:
except Exception as e:
    raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))
findings.ai_pattern: Overly broad exception catching with information disclosure

findings.title: /ask endpoint leaks internal error details via str(e)
findings.problem: Same pattern as api-007. Any exception from engine.query() is returned as-is to the client, potentially exposing LLM backend details, model paths, or internal architecture.
findings.fix: Return generic error message, log server-side: raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail='Error processing query')
findings.evidence:
except Exception as e:
    raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))
findings.ai_pattern: Overly broad exception catching with information disclosure

findings.title: /search endpoint leaks internal error details via str(e)
findings.problem: Same pattern as api-007 and api-008. Exceptions from engine.search_documents() returned directly to client.
findings.fix: Return generic error message, log server-side.
findings.evidence:
except Exception as e:
    raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))
findings.ai_pattern: Overly broad exception catching with information disclosure

findings.title: Display name passed unsanitized to engine.ingest_file
findings.problem: display_name (derived from sanitize_filename output) is passed as source_name to engine.ingest_file(). While sanitize_filename strips path components, the display_name still contains the original filename which could contain special characters, Unicode, or long strings that cause issues downstream in file path construction or logging.
findings.fix: Apply additional sanitization at point of use: truncate display_name, sanitize special characters for file system and logging contexts.

findings.title: IngestRequest.directory has no length or pattern validation
findings.problem: IngestRequest.directory is defined as bare str with no min_length, max_length, or pattern constraints. This allows empty strings, extremely long strings, or strings with control characters. The downstream validate_directory function handles some validation, but the Pydantic model should provide first-line defense.
findings.fix: Add constraints: directory: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=4096, pattern=r'^[\w\\/\-. ]+$')
findings.evidence:
class IngestRequest(BaseModel):
    directory: str  # No validation

findings.title: os.path.basename used instead of pathlib for cross-platform consistency
findings.problem: sanitize_filename at line 287 uses os.path.basename() while the rest of the file uses pathlib.Path objects. On some edge cases (e.g., trailing slashes, UNC paths), os.path.basename and Path().name may behave differently.
findings.fix: Use Path(filename).name for consistency with the rest of the codebase.

findings.title: Hardcoded default host and port
findings.problem: API_HOST defaults to '0.0.0.0' (line 659) and API_PORT defaults to 8080. While these are read from environment variables, the hardcoded defaults should come from a centralized config rather than being scattered in api_server.py.
findings.fix: Move defaults to app_paths.py or a config constants module alongside other application defaults.

findings.title: Broad except in lifespan could mask specific initialization failures
findings.problem: The except Exception at line 482 catches all exceptions during startup. While it logs the error, the generic RuntimeError message doesn't help diagnose which specific validation or initialization step failed.
findings.fix: Catch specific exceptions (ValueError for config errors, FileNotFoundError for missing model, ImportError for missing modules) separately.

findings.title: Temp file cleanup could raise exception in finally block
findings.problem: os.unlink() at line 629 could raise OSError (file already deleted by another process, permissions). The finally block doesn't handle cleanup failures, potentially masking the original exception or causing unhandled cleanup errors.
findings.fix: Wrap cleanup in try/except with logging: try: os.unlink(tmp_path) except OSError: logger.warning('Failed to cleanup temp file: %s', tmp_path)

findings.title: Inconsistent error response format across endpoints
findings.problem: Some endpoints return structured IngestResponse, others return raw dicts {'status': 'cleared', 'documents': [...], 'model_info': {...}}. No standardized error response model exists. API clients must handle different response schemas per endpoint.
findings.fix: Define a standard ApiResponse wrapper model and use it consistently across all endpoints.

findings.title: DNS resolution in URL validation could block or be slow
findings.problem: _resolve_and_validate_host() calls socket.getaddrinfo() which is a blocking call. On networks with slow DNS, this could cause the validation to hang. For a local-only API, this is low severity.
findings.fix: Add timeout to DNS resolution or use asyncio-compatible resolution.

findings.title: Print statements used instead of logger for startup/shutdown
findings.problem: Lines 480 and 486 use print() for startup/shutdown messages. These bypass structured logging and won't appear in log files in production.
findings.fix: Replace print() with logger.info() for consistent logging throughout the application.
findings.evidence:
print('Starting Document Q&A API Server...')
print('Shutting down...')
findings.ai_pattern: Print debugging left in production code
